completion_criteria_checked: Independently traced the code's actual execution order in scripts/process-communication-prefs-requests.ps1, line by line: malformed/missing-field/unrecognized-field requests are rejected before any state work begins; the full proposed project-state.json object is built in memory and validated against schemas/project-state.schema.json via Test-Json; only a passing validation leads to Set-Content on the real file. No code path was found where invalid input could be written before being checked.

No path found from malformed or unexpected input to a partial/corrupted write: every rejection point (malformed JSON, missing fields, unrecognized field names, schema failure) occurs strictly before any write. The post-write validate-cockpit-state.ps1 cross-check plus its rollback-on-failure safety net was independently confirmed as an additional safeguard, not the primary one.

Confirmed the consumer's recognized-field allowlist (tone, language_level, chattiness, no_cheerleading, concise_by_default, explicit_uncertainty, separate_facts_from_inference) is the only set of fields ever assigned, and only onto communication_prefs plus updated_at -- no other top-level project-state.json field is touched anywhere in the script, matching the task's own forbidden-scope boundary exactly.
evidence_reviewed: scripts/request-communication-prefs-update.ps1 (full file, read via remote repo access)
scripts/process-communication-prefs-requests.ps1 (full file, traced execution order in detail given this task's consequence level)
risks: Disclosed, non-blocking: the consumer does not validate incoming request files against schemas/request.schema.json itself, relying on lightweight structural checks instead. Reviewer judged this acceptable because the real safety concern -- validating the full proposed project state before writing it -- is genuinely and correctly done.

This verification was performed via the ChatGPT manual bridge (DECISION-086) with remote, read-only repo access only -- no local execution, no independent re-run of scripts/verify-handback-guardrails.ps1 or other local guardrails. Per docs/VERIFICATION_RESULT_SPEC.md, this is additive review, not a substitute for local-guardrail-based independent verification.
summary: Given this is the first request-driven canonical-state mutation in the project, the reviewer traced the consumer script's actual execution order line by line rather than trusting the worker's account. The critical safety claim -- full proposed-object schema validation strictly before any write to disk -- is genuinely true in the code, with no path found for invalid input to reach a partial or corrupted write. The field allowlist correctly confines all writes to communication_prefs and updated_at only. No blocker found.
next_action: Mark task complete. This completes Phase D3's currently-named scope (pcc-pathD-007 through 009) and docs/PATH_A_PLAN.md's entire currently-specified Category D roadmap. No further Category D task is currently named in the plan.
